summary: Epic Scarlet>Fire and soaring second‑set jams dominate
blurb: The second set erupts with a monster Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain stretch, followed by a thunderous Playing In The Band into China Doll and an expansive Space jam. Jerry’s lead work stays fierce despite an amp hiccup, while Phil and Brent lock in tight rhythm sections. Throwing Stones, Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad and One More Saturday Night round out the high‑energy finish.
review: Richmond Coliseum on October 6, 1984 delivered one of those rare shows where the second set simply takes over. After a solid first set, the band launched into an unforgettable Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence that reviewers called “hardcore” and “sick,” with Brent’s Caribbean‑flavored keys propelling Jerry’s string‑bending solo even after his amp sputtered. The momentum carried straight into a monster Playing In The Band that flowed seamlessly into China Doll, then exploded through Space where Phil’s bass bombs and the dual drummers created a deep, resonant soundscape before the band re‑assembled for Throwing Stones and a joyous post‑drums medley. Throughout the night Jerry’s guitar remained on fire, Phil’s low end was “clicking unreal,” Brent’s keyboards were both bright and inventive, and Bill & Mickey drove each jam with power and nuance. Highlights like Looks Like Rain in the first set and the encore Keep Your Day Job round out a show that many fans now cite as an underrated gem of the ’84 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Nine out of twelve recordings are positive, repeatedly praising the same moments – the Scarlet>Fire jam, Playing In The Band into China Doll, and the Space improvisation. Multiple sources call the performance "epic," "hardcore," and "locked in," while no more than one recording notes a negative aspect. This strong, consistent acclaim justifies a high positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with A+ audio quality, clear mids and strong bass; preserves the full power of the second‑set jams.

key_highlights: Monumental Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain jam that defines the night
High‑energy Playing In The Band into China Doll segue leading to an expansive Space jam
Post‑drums flow through Throwing Stones, Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad and Saturday Night

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a ferocious lead performance, breaking a string on Scarlet>Fire yet keeping the jam blazing; his amp quits during Space but he returns strong for Throwing Stones.
band_performance.Phil: Locks in with Brent delivering "clicking unreal" chemistry; drops ferocious bass bombs throughout the jams and steers the band during Jerry’s brief gear hiccup.
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid rhythm guitar work, especially on Scarlet>Fire, adding drive to the second‑set peaks.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work is highlighted as "sick," with marimba‑style lines and Caribbean flavor that fuel the Scarlet/Fire stretch; bright improvisation shines though sometimes sits forward in the mix.
band_performance.Mickey: Contributes deep, resonant textures during Space and helps power the second‑set energy.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers powerful double‑time feel on Scarlet>Fire and maintains a strong, driving presence throughout the jams.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
